purpose: Measured mutation-score floors for the hand-written modules Stryker mutates (generated src/** excluded), including the wrapper authenticated-client factory and final authenticated-dispatch boundary. Floors ratchet monotonic-up; raise only after a real test improvement. The checker compares an uncommitted worktree contract to committed HEAD; when the worktree matches HEAD, it compares to first-parent HEAD^1. A non-shallow repository may bootstrap only at a root commit or a first contract introduction proven from complete first-parent history; any shallow missing parent or missing first-parent contract and every unexpected Git/read/parse failure fail closed. Every predecessor governed package and module-floor path must remain present with well-formed numeric floors and cannot decrease; new packages and modules may be added. Positive mutate source paths and module floors must match exactly, resolve to repository files, and remain outside every configured exclusion; governed modules fail closed when a report contains zero covered mutants. A module floor may be zero only during an explicit, temporary calibration: its exact active source path must appear once in a non-empty calibrationPending list, and every pending source must have floor zero. Calibration entries must be removed when measured floors are raised. Mutation entrypoints generate the ignored manifest-derived runtime constants before Stryker starts; MCP builds its SDK workspace dependency and pins the four dedicated governed-module test files so Vitest discovery cannot silently omit result or risk coverage; and the dates property suite uses per-assertion fixed fast-check options so its score is repeatable. Phase 1 covers the wrapper hand-written modules. Phase 2 adds the MCP safety-critical modules (confirmation token store, centralized guarded registration, and governed risk registry) — Stryker's vitest-runner accepts vitest >=2.0.0, so the MCP suite is mutated on its now-unified Vitest 4 toolchain. dates.ts and webhook-url.ts floors were raised after Item 3 added month/weekday-name parse tests and the fe80::/10 [fec0::1]/[febf::1] boundary asserts.
